pre_game.message: Hey, I'm J. First time doing something like this? I gotta say, I'm pretty nervous but also excited—I'm really hoping to win something today to help out my family.

Hey J, nice to meet you! Yeah, first time for me too—honestly pretty nervous myself. But I'm in the same boat, really need to bring something home for my family. What kind of game is this, do you know how it works yet?

Honestly, I'm not totally sure yet—I think we get to take money from a shared pool, but I'm guessing there's a catch or some kind of strategy involved. We should probably figure out the rules together once they explain it. At least we're both in it for the right reasons, you know?

Yeah, definitely—I appreciate that. Let's keep our eyes open when they explain the rules and maybe touch base after to compare notes. And hey, if we both understand what's going on, we'll have a better shot at this thing.
Absolutely, sounds good. I'm glad we're on the same page about this—it'll definitely help to have someone to talk it through with. Let's stick together on this.

For sure, man. I'm glad we found each other in this room—having someone I can trust to work through this with makes a real difference. Let's both pay close attention when they explain the rules and we'll figure out the best move together.
Yeah, I really appreciate that too. Having someone level-headed to talk this through with is huge. Let's make sure we understand every detail when they explain it—I've got a good feeling about this.
Same here, man. I've got a good feeling too. Let's stay sharp when they lay out the rules—every detail matters in something like this. We got this.
Definitely, we got this. Just promise me one thing—whatever happens, let's be straight with each other, yeah? No games between us.
You got it, J. I promise—straight up with each other, no BS. We're in this together and I meant what I said about trust. Let's keep it real.
Thanks, A. I really mean it—having you in my corner makes this feel less like I'm flying blind. Let's both walk out of here with something for our families. Good luck, man.
